title: erica hispidula var. serpyllifolia
intro: Erica hispidula var. serpyllifolia, also known as the dwarf heath or mountain heath, is a low-growing, flowering plant species within the heath family (Ericaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erica hispidula var. serpyllifolia include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. The plant is typically prepared as a tea infusion, tincture, or dried extract, with the leaves and flowers being the primary components used in herbal preparations.
